signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (most accommodative in G8), any RBNZ hawkish delivery or signal triggers a crowded short-unwind. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and IRGC drone activity introduce risk-off headwinds, but the immediate catalyst remains the RBNZ policy decision. NZD/USD at 0.5847.

signals.title: CHF/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: CHF outperforming as the G8 leader (+0.278%) while NZD lags (-0.675%), creating a 0.953% spread. Despite a -225bp carry deficit for CHF/NZD (SNB 0.0% vs RBNZ 2.25%), safe-haven demand for CHF is dominant following reports of Iran downing a US-made MQ-9 drone. Carry advantage for NZD is currently secondary to geopolitical risk-off flows. Watch for VIX > 25 to accelerate the carry unwind.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.12 supported by +300bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). Options market signals caution with a -1.41 25d RR (moderate put skew) despite the carry bid, suggesting institutional hedging against BoJ intervention as price holds near the 160.00 threshold. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 remains crowded; intervention risk rises on a sustained test of 160.00.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.3% creates a +2.8pt VRP, signaling elevated hedging demand despite a neutral 25d RR (-0.35). USD CESI at +24 (strong positive) vs EUR at +5 suggests US data outperformance continues to anchor the pair. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) carry favors USD, keeping EUR/USD upside structurally limited at 1.1646.
